explanation.summary: Carbs are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in S tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in S tier with an overall score of 54 from a base nutrition score of 50. Context lifted it by 4 points.

topPros.title: elite micronutrient density
topPros.explanation: This is exactly the kind of nutrient-packed vegetable the category is supposed to reward.

topPros.title: very light for the volume
topPros.explanation: A practical plus for a maintenance-and-repair food.

topPros.title: easy to use in lots of meals
topPros.explanation: Works raw, cooked, blended, and as a side or add-in.

topCons.title: contains anti-nutrients
topCons.explanation: Oxalates can reduce mineral usefulness in some contexts.

topCons.title: volume shrinks a lot when cooked
topCons.explanation: Can make portions feel smaller than expected.

topCons.title: not useful as a fuel food
topCons.explanation: That is fine for vegetables, but it does limit some use cases.

narrationNotes: Context improved the final tier case by 4 points.

closing.summary: Spinach has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best when you want low downside and easy micronutrient support.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want low downside and easy micronutrient support.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?
